message:
fix(vsdk): validate expected_output_hash is well-formed SHA-256 hex before capsule replay

validate_manifest() previously only checked that expected_output_hash was
non-empty, allowing arbitrary strings like "wrong_hash" through. This
caused misleading divergence results during replay — the capsule would
appear to diverge when the real issue was a malformed manifest.

Changes:
- Add is_sha256_hex() helper (64 char hex validation)
- validate_manifest() now returns ManifestIncomplete if expected_output_hash
  is not a valid 64-character hex SHA-256 digest
- test_validate_manifest_malformed_expected_hash: verifies malformed hash
  is rejected at validation time
- test_replay_capsule_rejects_malformed_expected_hash: verifies replay
  rejects malformed hash via manifest validation
- Fix existing tests (test_replay_capsule_diverged, test_seal_session_with_failure)
  to use valid hex strings ("f".repeat(64)) instead of "wrong_hash"/"wrong"
  which now fail validation before reaching the divergence check

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
